coverage.notes: Universal finite-constant lane for the dedicated Section 4 constant theorems. `subsidy_u128_safety_proved` closes the §19.1 subsidy arithmetic safety ceiling, and `htlc_timelock_proved` closes the strict refund-before-expiry timelock semantics. This split intentionally stops short of the live witness-length validator surface and the Section 4 native binding-manifest fragment. Those two surfaces now live in dedicated scoped rows rather than depressing the dedicated constant lane.
limitations: This row does not claim the legacy/pre-rotation `validateWitnessItemLengths` branch surface. That live witness-length subset is tracked separately in `consensus_constants_witness_lengths_pre_rotation`.
Other Section 4 literals that already belong to dedicated rows such as weight accounting or DA integrity remain outside this finite-constant lane and are not re-counted here.

This row does not claim the Section 4 normative cross-reference that makes the native registry tuple the native component of the chain-instance canonical binding manifest under Section 23.2.2. That pre-rotation native-binding fragment is tracked separately in `consensus_constants_native_binding_manifest_pre_rotation`.

coverage.notes: Universal row for the authoritative pre-rotation witness-length fragment. `validateWitnessItemLengths_eq_registry_pre_rotation` closes the bridge from the legacy hardcoded validator to `validateWitnessItemLengthsRegistry PRE_ROTATION_REGISTRY`; the retained registry companion theorems close unknown-suite rejection, sentinel rejection/acceptance, and the three ML-DSA-87 rejection paths on that live validator. The removed acceptance/exhaustiveness wrappers are not part of the active imported source obligation.
limitations: Scope is `validateWitnessItemLengthsRegistry Rotation.PRE_ROTATION_REGISTRY` only. This row does not claim arbitrary post-rotation registries, additional native suites, or release-time registry drift handling beyond the singleton pre-rotation registry.

Registry-aware parse-stage witness classification and registered-suite canonicalization in `TxParseV2.parseWitnessItemWithRegistry` are still intentionally out of scope for this Section 4 witness-length fragment.

This row does not re-count the dedicated finite constants carried by `consensus_constants` or the Section 4 native binding-manifest fragment carried by `consensus_constants_native_binding_manifest_pre_rotation`.

coverage.section_heading: ## 4. Consensus Constants (Native Binding Manifest Pre-Rotation Residual)
coverage.notes: Universal row for the pre-rotation singleton native binding-manifest fragment added by spec commit `a04f543`. `fi_rot_03_pre_rotation_ml_dsa_resolves` fixes the authoritative pre-rotation lookup to `ML_DSA_87_ENTRY`; `pre_rotation_registered_iff` closes the default registration surface on `PRE_ROTATION_REGISTRY`; the quantified `ml_dsa_87_params_bridge` closes the default parameter surface for any singleton registry extensionally equal to `[ML_DSA_87_ENTRY]`; `ml_dsa_87_manifest_tuple_bridge` closes the exact Section 4.1.1 native tuple including `semantic_id` and `binding_profile`; `ml_dsa_87_manifest_bytes_hash_bridge` closes the exact `NativeSuiteEntryBytes_v1` payload and corresponding `registry_entry_hash`; and `validateWitnessItemLengths_eq_registry_pre_rotation` bridges the legacy live witness-length validator to the registry-specialized helper on `PRE_ROTATION_REGISTRY`. The deleted aggregate binding wrapper is not claimed.
limitations: Scope is the singleton pre-rotation native registry fragment only. The counted manifest theorems are universal over any singleton registry extensionally equal to `[ML_DSA_87_ENTRY]`, not over arbitrary post-rotation registries, arbitrary additional native suites, or the full Section 4 `NativeSuiteEntry` production profile lifecycle.

This row closes the native tuple / bytes / hash fragment and the pre-signature live binding slice for the default ML-DSA-87 entry, but it does not by itself claim full native verifier cryptographic correctness beyond the authoritative `binding_profile` token and the supporting live/bridge theorems already counted here.

This row also does not claim the `CORE_EXT` profile-set half of the chain-instance canonical binding manifest, manifest publication/governance ownership, or release-time runtime-table drift checks. Those remain outside the current formal Section 4 theorem surface.
CV-PARSE/CV-COMPACT replay is fixture-bound Lean evidence; any correspondence between Lean serialization and Go/Rust remains human-reviewed.
coverage.notes: Universal Lean serializer-contract lane: CompactSize canonicality/overflow/cursor-advance theorems, fixed-width primitive encoder size/roundtrip theorems, explicit between-contracts for input/output/witness-item/witness-list/DA-core lanes, and `parseTx_result_integrity`. The removed upper full-transaction serializer modules are not claimed. CV-PARSE and CV-COMPACT are complementary fixture-bound Lean replay, not machine-checked Lean-to-Go/Rust equivalence.

limitations: This row proves only the structural/live serializer boundary: `serializeTxCore` versus `serializeTx`, the witness-empty lane, and the tagged txid/wtxid preimage-domain separation. It does not claim that equal emitted digests are impossible; that explicit SHA3 ceiling now lives in the separate crypto residual row.

CV-PARSE and CV-SIG replay remain complementary executable evidence for the concrete emitted txid/wtxid bytes on shipped fixtures. That replay lane does not upgrade this row into a universal cross-language byte-equivalence claim outside the exercised vectors.
coverage.notes: Universal theorem lane: `transaction_identifiers_proved` lifts the section statement onto the live canonical serializer boundary (`serializeTxCore` vs `serializeTx`), `txid_wtxid_witness_empty_serialization_shape` closes the witness-empty lane explicitly via the retained `CompactSize(0)` marker, and `txid_wtxid_identifier_domain_contract` keeps the structural and tagged-domain boundaries explicit on the canonical serializer outputs. Separate fixture lane: CV-PARSE and CV-SIG replay for the concrete emitted txid/wtxid bytes on the shipped vectors. That executable lane is complementary evidence only and does not replace the universal theorem lane. The SHA3 collision ceiling is tracked separately in `transaction_identifiers_crypto_residual` rather than depressing this structural/live row.

limitations: This residual row intentionally covers only the crypto ceiling left after the universal `transaction_identifiers` split. It does not re-count the structural serializer/domain distinctness already carried by the universal row.

The counted theorem is a reduction theorem: equal live canonical txid/wtxid digests imply a SHA3-256 collision on distinct executable preimages, but it does not itself prove such collisions impossible without the explicit SHA3-256 collision / second-preimage resistance assumption.
coverage.notes: Assumption-backed residual row for the txid/wtxid crypto ceiling. `txid_wtxid_digest_collision_reduces_to_sha3_collision` packages the honest remaining boundary after the structural/live split: if the canonical live txid and wtxid digests for the same transaction are equal, SHA3-256 collides on distinct executable preimages. Keeping this theorem in its own row prevents the universal serializer/domain theorem lane from inheriting the explicit crypto assumption.

Universal theorem lane: full Except-chain weight closure, where `txWeightAndStats_ok_weight_eq_constrained` records the successful parseTxHeader + parseTxBody + finalizeTxWeight chain and proves the resulting `.ok` stats weight matches `computeWeight` on parse-derived witnesses. Separate fixture lane: CV-WEIGHT replay on the shipped vectors. That executable lane is complementary regression evidence only and is not the basis for the universal ceiling. Section inventory still contains 29 counted theorem refs across both lanes.

Active evidence is limited to the canonical executable `checkWitnessCommitment` stage reduction after parse/pow/target/linkage/merkle gates, the coinbase-slot rewrite, and CV-BLOCK-BASIC replay. No registered theorem establishes the exact-one matching-anchor fallback criterion. The merkle-tag anti-alias SHA3 ceiling is tracked separately in `witness_commitment_crypto_residual`.
limitations: Executable stage and fixture evidence do not prove the exact-one matching-anchor fallback criterion.
A formal re-proof of that criterion is deferred under RUB-1033.
Cross-domain txid/wtxid tagged-root anti-aliasing is not claimed in this row and is tracked separately in `witness_commitment_crypto_residual`.

coverage.notes: Dedicated assumption-backed residual for the witness-commitment anti-alias boundary. The counted merkle-tag reduction theorems show that any cross-domain root equality between txid tags (0x00/0x01) and witness-merkle tags (0x02/0x03) would require a SHA3-256 collision on distinct tagged preimages. This row intentionally carries only that crypto ceiling and does not re-count the separately scoped `witness_commitment` row.
limitations: Ruling out txid/wtxid tagged-root aliasing remains assumption-backed: the row reduces any such equality to a SHA3-256 collision obligation on distinct tagged preimages.
The merkle-tag theorems are reduction theorems (equality implies a SHA3 collision on distinct tagged preimages), not an axiom-free cryptographic impossibility proof.

coverage.notes: Lean structural selector/base-type model plus CV-SIGHASH fixture evidence; this is not a live Go/Rust bridge or full-protocol residual claim.
limitations: The executable digestV1 replay path covers only the current fixture subset, not arbitrary raw transaction pairs.
DA kinds lack an active Lean live bridge; no live Go/Rust DigestV1 or full-protocol residual claim is registered.

coverage.notes: Assumption-backed digest ceiling: the registered theorems reduce successful `digestV1` output equality and an output collision to SHA3-256 preimage/collision obligations. They do not establish cryptographic collision impossibility.
limitations: These are reduction theorems under the SHA3-256 cryptographic ceiling, not an axiom-free proof that collisions cannot occur.
This residual does not expand the bounded SIGHASH trace contract into a live Go/Rust implementation-equivalence claim.

coverage.notes: Universal theorem lane: block-level 6-stage live error propagation on `validateBlockBasic`, direct live tx-parse and tx-semantic stage bridges, and the retained semantic ordering/injectivity contract. The experimental `tx_parse_pipeline_deterministic` model omits DA-core and payload-read failures and is intentionally unregistered as complete live parse evidence. TARGET ambiguity is formally resolved via `target_error_disambiguated`, and `dispatchCovenantValidation` is claimed on the direct live per-input path rather than through a parallel-model helper caveat. Separate fixture lane: CV-VALIDATION-ORDER replay on pinned ordering vectors. That executable lane is complementary regression evidence only and does not replace the live stage-order theorem surface.

limitations: Scope: static Lean model/helper for bounded u16 tag disposition only; no live implementation bridge is claimed. This row does not claim output acceptance, activation or deployment behavior, descriptor semantics, spend rules, cryptographic semantics, or client equivalence.

The retained validateOutGenesis evidence consists of canonical accepted samples, unknown/reserved rejection theorems, and CV-COVENANT-GENESIS replay for the legacy genesis-model surface. It does not claim a universal acceptance characterization and does not cover CORE_STEALTH or CORE_SIMPLICITY.
coverage.notes: Primary §14 static Lean-model/helper lane: `covenantDispositionComplete` machine-checks the exact static disposition for every u16 tag: the named accepted tags, reserved 0x00FF, deployment-gated CORE_SIMPLICITY at 0x0106, explicit invalid 0x0001 and 0x0102, and every other u16 tag invalid with TX_ERR_COVENANT_TYPE_INVALID as the registry error marker. The 0x0102 entry is reserved only as a non-executable registry annotation, remains invalid and unassigned, and has no activation, support, or executable semantics. This is tag disposition only and not a live implementation bridge. The retained canonical accepted-sample and rejection theorems plus CV-COVENANT-GENESIS replay are bounded legacy genesis-model evidence; they neither define activation or descriptor behavior nor cover CORE_STEALTH or CORE_SIMPLICITY.

Universal theorem lane: §15 retarget formula closure with 6 constant pins, identity/zero cases, lo≤hi under valid preconditions, strengthened clamped range `[lo, hi]`, candidate monotonicity (both `tActual` and `targetOldNat`), 4x stability boundary, timestamp clamping bounds, max retarget ratio, and 4 LIVE theorems on the `retargetV1` monadic function (parse/validation error paths + `pattern=None` success). Separate fixture lane: CV-POW conformance replay on shipped vectors. That executable lane is complementary evidence only and does not replace the universal theorem lane. Helper `nat_div_le_div_right` is proved from `Nat.div_add_mod`.

Section 16 claim is carried by concrete regression theorems plus the retained registry-aware rejection and threshold/vault theorems on `validateWitnessItemLengthsRegistry`, `validateThresholdSigSpendRegistry`, and `validateVaultSpendRegistry` specialized to `PRE_ROTATION_REGISTRY`. The deleted aggregate binding and ML-DSA acceptance wrappers are not claimed. This row closes the named retained spend-side helper/live cases only; parser-stage witness canonicalization and activation-stage suite gating remain outside this surface.
limitations: Registry-aware coverage is specialized to PRE_ROTATION_REGISTRY; post-rotation registries with additional suites remain future scope.
The parse-time structural validity / registry validity / activation validity split introduced for the witness parser model is not itself counted in this Section 16 row.

coverage.notes: Universal theorem lane: the exact live duplicate-replay path, where `ReplayDomainBehavioral.lean` keeps the parseTx nonce-determinism / duplicate-nonce bridge and `ReplayDomainUniversal.lean` proves the executable `anyDuplicate` helper is extensionally equivalent to the abstract `nonceReplayFree` invariant and that the live `nonceReplayCheck` accepts exactly replay-free nonce lists and rejects duplicate-bearing nonce lists. Separate fixture lane: CV-REPLAY on shipped fixtures and block-basic replay examples. That executable lane remains complementary evidence only and does not replace the universal theorem lane. Broader chain-wide/state-threaded replay semantics stay outside this section's own statement rather than as a residual limitation of the row.

Block connection pipeline with coinbase, value conservation, and per-tx state machine. Current CORE_VAULT spend proofs remain registered; the retained experimental VaultStateMachine lifecycle corpus is not current-protocol evidence. TxContext parameters are caller-provided. Coinbase UTXO marking is covered by the current list-to-RBMap bridge and query-level semantics. Vault-policy replay is limited to the safe subset/default-order path.
limitations: RBMap-level operational equivalence is not fully proved end-to-end; the current closure relies on the explicit list-to-RBMap bridge and query-level semantics now available in CoinbaseBehavioral.
vault_policy_default_order_safe_proved covers the spend-side safe subset / default-order path only and is not a full L1↔L2 equivalence theorem for every vault-policy branch.

coverage.notes: Universal theorem lane: subsidy arithmetic and coinbase block-connection closure via `subsidy_u128_safety_proved`, `SubsidyV1.connectBlock_end_to_end_proved`, `blockSubsidy_bounded`, `blockSubsidy_in_u64`, and `subsidy_accumulation_in_u128`. Separate fixture lane: CV-SUBSIDY replay on the shipped subsidy vectors. That executable lane is complementary regression evidence only and does not replace the universal theorem lane.

Universal theorem lane: value conservation at single-tx level (`sum_in = sum_out + fee` via `scanInputs` / `sumOutputs`) and block level (`connectBlockTxs` preserves conservation across all txs), backed by the current `utxo_apply_basic_universal_refinement` bridge on the real UTXO-map model rather than an abstract ledger. Separate fixture lane: CV-SUBSIDY replay on the shipped vectors. That executable lane is complementary regression evidence only and does not replace the universal theorem lane.

Universal theorem lane: S21 DA set integrity closure, where `validateDASetIntegrity_ok_constrained` decomposes `.ok` into all 4 parse-derived stages (`accumulateDATxs`, batch count bound, orphan check, verify integrity). Separate fixture lane: CV-DA-INTEGRITY replay on the shipped vectors. That executable lane is complementary regression evidence only and does not replace the universal theorem lane. Section inventory still contains 72 counted theorem refs across both lanes.

Universal theorem lane: `medianTimePast_value` plus `medianTimePast_index_valid` close the list-to-MTP extraction path on the live helper, `timestampBounds_*_iff` characterizes the canonical old/future/ok boundary, and the new constrained LIVE bridge theorems lift that boundary to the real `validateBlockBasicCheck` path once the parse/pow/target/linkage/merkle/witness prefix has passed. The top-level counted surface now includes exact acceptance and old/future rejection theorems on the live block-basic path rather than concrete regression wrappers. Separate fixture lane: CV-TIMESTAMP replay on shipped timestamp-bounds and block-basic vectors. That executable lane remains complementary evidence only and does not replace the universal theorem lane.
limitations: The counted LIVE bridge theorems are constrained to the real `validateBlockBasicCheck` path after parse/pow/target/linkage/merkle/witness prefix success, and the accept theorem additionally assumes nonce replay passes on the remaining suffix. This row does not claim unrelated block-basic stages beyond that covered timestamp gate.
No chain-wide timestamp monotonicity claim beyond the covered `timestampBounds` gate and current replayed integration path is asserted here.

coverage.notes: Current Lean model evidence establishes deterministic selection for represented chainwork/hash values: `forkSelect_exhaustive` covers the model cases, `forkSelect_equal_chains` covers equal represented values, `forkSelect_total_det` covers model-distinguishable pairs, and `bytesLT_antisym` plus `bytesLT_total_of_ne` provide the tie-break properties. Separate generated fixture evidence: `fork_choice_select_cv_contract_proved` machine-checks exactly the six current positive protocol-generated CV-FORK-CHOICE fork_choice_select vectors in their pinned order. Distinct candidates with equal work and equal 32-byte hash are not distinguished by this model; candidate identity/hash-collision exclusion is an unproved premise tracked by RUB-1033. `compiler_trusted` records a `Lean.ofReduceBool` compiler/evaluator dependency in the registered fixture contract; it does not assert absence of ordinary Lean foundations.
limitations: forkSelect models the runtime fork_choice_select. Go/Rust code follows identical if/else chain. Structural equivalence evident from inspection but not proved via rfl (Go/Rust not importable to Lean).
The 32-byte hash length premise (hL/hR) is a protocol type invariant (SHA3-256 output), not a semantic precondition. Runtime block hashes are always 32 bytes by construction.
Distinct candidates with equal work and equal 32-byte hash are not distinguished; candidate identity and hash-collision exclusion are unproved premises deferred under RUB-1033.

The generated Lean contract covers six positive fork_choice_select rows only. Negative expect_ok=false rows are runtime conformance-only, and standalone-only extra vectors are outside this registry claim.

coverage.notes: The retained theorem establishes totality for the BlockValidationOrder model: every input produces the model acceptance witness or an error returned by `validateBlockBasic`.
limitations: This model totality theorem does not prove the normative gate order or error-precedence requirements.
A full order/error-precedence re-proof is deferred under RUB-1033.

coverage.notes: The counted theorem is `accept_reject_equivalence_live`: for one identical precheck result and signature-task list, sequential reduction and the live SigCheckQueue.Flush accept/reject helper contract return the same verdict. Helper/model reducer lemmas remain in ParallelEquivalence.lean as supporting mathematics rather than separate coverage.
limitations: No theorem composes `accept_reject_equivalence_live` with `connectBlockFullComputed*` into end-to-end sequential-versus-parallel block equality. The retained caller-supplied ConnectBlockFull wrapper lemmas are experimental and unregistered.

Auxiliary tagged-result lowest-index theorems remain in ParallelEquivalence.lean for helper reasoning only; they are not counted here because the live queue may early-abort and surface a deterministic submission-order error instead of the absolute lowest failing index.

coverage.notes: §14 closure over TxContext helper construction, value conservation, and continuing-output bounds. The 22 counted theorems cover sortExtIds, buildTxContext, buildTxContextFromValues, buildTxContextLive, compareUint128, validateContinuingOutputCount, and value conservation. The retained caller-supplied ConnectBlockFull computed-wrapper lemmas are experimental and unregistered; they do not establish a transaction/UTXO-derived live TxContext. The retired CORE_EXT/CV-TXCTX sighash allowlist policy is not an active premise or claim. Zero fixture dependencies; source syntax is reviewed separately and does not override compiler_trusted/Lean.ofReduceBool closure.
limitations: No registered theorem derives TxContextData from the connected transactions or UTXOs; the retained ConnectBlockFull computed wrapper surface is model-only.

coverage.notes: Universal for §4.1.2/§4.1.3/§23.2.1 native crypto suite rotation. native_rotation_ok_constrained proves that for any well-formed descriptor in any well-formed registry, at every height: the phase partition holds and all active create/spend suites resolve to unique entries. Covers all 5 rotation phases including post-rotation dispatch. No claim about byte-level wire encoding trace (G7 residual).
limitations: The model is machine-checked in Lean; correspondence to Go/Rust is human-reviewed, and byte-level wire encoding trace remains unformalized (G7 residual).

coverage.notes: Universal closure of the descriptor-aware spend-side live gate bridge. validateP2PKSpendPreSig_ok_constrained and scanSingleInputStep_ok_constrained prove that, once the same preceding live structural guards have passed, the live P2PK spend helper and the live input-scan P2PK branch succeed iff the committed suite is active in NativeSpendSuites(h,d). Paired rejection theorems fix the exact inactive-suite live errors.
limitations: Scope: descriptor-aware P2PK spend-side live gate bridge only; create-side gate and other spend validators are tracked in separate rows.

coverage.section_heading: ## Threshold Spend Pre-Rotation Suite Gate Bridge
coverage.notes: Pre-rotation-only bridge for the threshold-sig per-item comparison in validateThresholdSigSpendNoCrypto. The registered theorems bind only the no-descriptor singleton ML-DSA-87 case; descriptor-present and post-rotation behavior is excluded and fails closed.
limitations: Per-item pre-rotation comparison only; threshold counting and all descriptor-present/post-rotation behavior remain outside this row.
No independent stealth live model or Go/Rust parity claim is registered.

coverage.notes: Universal closure for the real HTLC spend-side structural/live lane only. The new LIVE theorems sit directly on `dispatchCovenantValidation` and `validateHTLCSpendNoCrypto`, proving branch routing into the HTLC path, fixed two-slot witness cursor advancement, sentinel/path-shape parse rejection, claim-prefix parse rejection, unknown-path rejection, refund timelock enforcement with explicit precedence before later signature checks, and positive refund-path acceptance once the live structural guards pass.
limitations: Scope: live structural HTLC spend-side only. Claim-path preimage/hash equality, hashlock crypto semantics, and broader cryptographic guarantees remain outside this row and belong to the dedicated assumption-backed HTLC crypto follow-up.

coverage.section_heading: ## Create-Side Live Gate (Top-Level Runtime P2PK Path)
coverage.notes: Dedicated counted LIVE/BRIDGE row for the exact output-level runtime create validator `validateOutGenesisWithRotation`. The counted surface starts at the real top-level P2PK branch dispatch, not an extracted inner helper. `validateOutGenesisWithRotation_some_p2pk_ok_iff` proves that on the descriptor-aware branch `.ok ()` is equivalent to the full live P2PK prefix at that surface: `covenantType = COV_TYPE_P2PK`, `value ≠ 0`, `covenantData.size = 33`, and suite membership in `NativeCreateSuites(h,d)`. The paired rejection theorem fixes inactive-suite failure as `TX_ERR_SIG_ALG_INVALID` on that same top-level live path. `validateOutGenesisWithRotation_eq_validateOutGenesis_of_not_p2pk` proves zero blast radius outside the P2PK branch, and the `none` corollaries characterize the exact pre-rotation singleton fallback (`ML-DSA-87`) with the same top-level error ordering.
limitations: Scope: exact output-level create validator only; tx-level nil-transaction handling and loop-over-outputs behavior from Go `ValidateTxCovenantsGenesis` remain outside this row.
Other create-time covenant semantics stay owned by their dedicated rows; this row closes the runtime P2PK branch and its delegation boundary, not the entire covenant registry theorem surface.

coverage.notes: Dedicated assumption-backed HTLC claim-path crypto lane. The counted theorem `claim_hash_collision_reduces_to_sha3_collision` now sits on the extracted claim-path hashlock helper `validateHTLCClaimHashlock`, which isolates the preimage/hashlock slice after the structural claim-path guards already separated from the main HTLC row. If two distinct executable claim preimages both satisfy that helper for the same covenant hashlock target, then SHA3-256 would have to collide on distinct inputs. This row intentionally records reduction evidence only and does not overclaim an axiom-free cryptographic impossibility proof or a full live spend-path closure.
limitations: Structural branch selection outside the claim selector, refund-path semantics, and the later shared `sigItem` checks remain owned by other rows; this row covers the extracted claim-path selector plus hashlock slice only.
Cryptographic impossibility (collision/second-preimage resistance for SHA3-256) remains an external assumption boundary; this row provides reduction evidence only.

coverage.notes: Universal closure of §23 feature activation FSM / flag-day surface. FeatureActivationLiveBridge bridges the prechecked state-only live multi-boundary state-at-height fold to the existing fold-based FSM model, then lifts multi_step_monotone onto that reachable helper path after deployment validation (`startHeight ≤ timeoutHeight`) and the sufficient window_signal_counts prefix guard. Single-step priority/no-skip/iff theorems plus flagday_complete_behavior remain complementary executable evidence. 14 theorems total.
limitations: The state-at-height bridge applies only after live deployment validation (`startHeight ≤ timeoutHeight`) and sufficient window_signal_counts. Invalid deployments reject before the fold and are outside the state-only theorem surface.

coverage_summary.counting_rule: theorem_references counts every row reference; unique_theorem_names counts each fully-qualified theorem once; reused_theorem_names lists names referenced by more than one row.

allowed: Lean executable semantics replay the non-CV-PV conformance fixtures represented by modules imported by RubinFormal.Conformance.Index; runtime/parallel-only CV-PV gates remain outside Lean replay scope
Lean evidence is op-scoped and mixed; any correspondence to Go/Rust is human-reviewed rather than machine-checked implementation equivalence

The registry currently covers 32 machine-checked coverage entries: 25 proved, 4 proved_with_axiom, and 3 stated. Evidence levels comprise 24 universal, 4 assumption-backed, 3 model, and 1 contract entries.
formal claims are bounded by explicit forbidden claims below

Bridge theorems (LIVE/BRIDGE class) prove properties of Lean transcriptions of Go/Rust functions. Lean-to-Go/Rust parity is verified by human code review, not machine-checked. The behavioral claim applies to the Lean transcription; transfer to Go/Rust binary relies on the reviewed parity.
forbidden: formal verification of full RUBIN consensus / CANONICAL semantics
cryptographic security proofs of ML-DSA / non-native candidate suites / SHA3-256
bit-exact wire/serialization proof beyond what is exercised by conformance fixtures
treating formal-only extra theorems as additional pinned-section coverage when they are not in the registry
universal mechanized refinement between spec text and Go/Rust implementations
claiming machine-checked Lean-to-Go/Rust equivalence for bridge theorems — Lean cannot import Go/Rust; parity is human-verified by code review

status_taxonomy.machine_checked_universal: Universal proof scope over all quantified inputs, independent of fixtures. Row-level proof_trust records whether compiled closures include Lean.ofReduceBool compiler/evaluator behavior: kernel_checked excludes that dependency only, not ordinary Lean foundations such as propext, Quot.sound, or Classical.choice.
status_taxonomy.machine_checked_behavioral: Behavioral decomposition of a Lean transcription of a live Go/Rust formula into pure functions with full constant pins, monotonicity, and CV replay. Not monadic end-to-end. Lean-to-Go/Rust parity is human-verified by code review, not machine-checked.
status_taxonomy.machine_checked_contract: Fixture-backed contract: conformance vectors or Go-trace vectors replayed via native_decide. Sound for covered inputs only; not a universal proof.
status_taxonomy.machine_checked_model: Property of a Lean model or helper definition with no live bridge to Go/Rust implementation. Honest about coverage gap; should be upgraded to behavioral or universal when a bridge is added.
status_taxonomy.machine_checked_assumption_backed: Universal reduction or proof that depends on one or more explicitly named assumptions. Sound only if those assumptions hold.
